name: Penn Federal Credit Union
description_short: Penn Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution based in Philadelphia offering personal loans, mortgages, credit cards, and deposit accounts with a focus on personalized member service.
description_long: Penn Federal Credit Union operates as a federally chartered credit union headquartered at 3131 Walnut Street in Philadelphia, PA. The institution emphasizes its identity as a member-owned cooperative rather than a profit-driven bank, with testimonials dating back to founding members in 1979. The credit union positions itself as an alternative to traditional banking, leveraging personal relationships and community orientation as core differentiators. The credit union offers a range of financial products including personal loans, mortgages, credit cards (with Visa cards starting with 4169 and 4052), debit cards, checking and savings accounts, CDs, and share draft accounts. Members can access online banking through Sprig Mobile Banking, utilize Co-Op Shared Branching for access to other credit union branches, and leverage their non-surcharge ATM network through CU$. The institution provides student loan options through Sallie Mae partnerships and insurance products via TruStage. Members can make loan payments online through their website payment portal with a $5.99 convenience fee alternative to late fees. Penn Federal distinguishes itself through emphasis on personalized member relationships, with staff reportedly knowing members by name and providing tailored financial guidance for mortgages, auto loans, vacation financing, tuition assistance, and computer loans. The credit union recently expanded with a new Spruce Street Branch location. Member testimonials specifically praise loan rates, customer service quality, and staff accessibility for financial planning advice. The institution maintains dual contact channels through a main branch (267-969-7011) and newer Spruce Street branch (267-534-5091). As a federally insured credit union, Penn Federal offers member protections inherent to the credit union model. However, the website provides limited information about specific loan terms, APR ranges, membership eligibility requirements, or detailed product comparisons. The institution serves a Philadelphia-based community with physical branch locations rather than operating as a digital-only service. Like most credit unions, membership may be restricted to specific employer groups or geographic areas, though this is not explicitly detailed on their website.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Personal member relationships with staff who know members by name and their financial needs
Competitive rates on personal loans and mortgages according to member testimonials
Expanded accessibility with new Spruce Street Branch location
Non-surcharge ATM access through CU$ network
Multiple contact methods with dual branch phone lines (main and Spruce Street)
Online payment portal with $5.99 convenience fee alternative to $20 late fees
Co-Op Shared Branching for access to other credit union locations
cons: Limited publicly available information about specific loan terms, rates, or APR ranges on website
Membership eligibility criteria not clearly stated on website
Physical branch-based model with limited digital-only options compared to neobanks
No information provided about minimum deposit requirements or account opening process
Customer service availability appears limited to business hours with no 24/7 support mentioned
best_for: Philadelphia-area employees or community members seeking personalized member service and relationship banking
Borrowers prioritizing loan products with competitive rates backed by personal member advisory
Credit union members seeking shared branching access through the Co-Op network
diagnosis: Penn Federal Credit Union is best for Philadelphia-area residents or employees seeking relationship-based banking with competitive loan rates and personalized member service. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility, specific loan terms, and APR ranges are not detailed on their website, requiring direct contact to determine qualification and product terms.
services: Credit cards (Visa cards with two product lines)
Online bill payment and loan payment portal
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
